gaps: term_year grouping returns no buckets for pre-modern SCOTUS dockets (no filed dates in the corpus), so era-specific base rates were unavailable
no way to filter stats/query to cert-stage petition outcomes specifically; the resolved-label mix ('other'-dominated) is hard to interpret for a petition event
semantic/topic retrieval over historical SCOTUS priors would beat the structured filters here (no topic, judges, or citations on this stub docket to filter by)
helpful: fedcourts paths resolved the cell's I/O locations instantly
fedcourts stats gave the resolved-label distribution, which calibrated how much to trust the corpus's outcome labels
notes: Corpus query/stats ran fine but added little for this cell: the docket is a sparse historical stub and the decisive signals (consolidated merits docket numbers, linked opinion cluster, recognized caption) were all in the snapshot itself.
